title: Lifecycle Output Surface Classification
goal: Make root lifecycle output classify relevant surfaces by ownership, action, and reason so agents do not infer lifecycle safety from prose or ad hoc lists.
non_goals: Do not automatically remove optional legacy surfaces.
Do not support every historical product shape.
Do not add a new top-level command.

intent.outcome: Root lifecycle JSON exposes predictable surface classifications for #482 acceptance classes.
intent.constraints: Keep text compact and route detail to JSON; preserve root lifecycle front-door ownership.
intent.latitude: Implement classification as derived lifecycle output over existing module reports and root summaries.
intent.escalation: Stop before broad module rewrites or destructive policy changes.

execution.next_step: Add derived lifecycle surface classifications and tests for dry-run/apply symmetry.
execution.proof: Run workspace CLI tests, proof selector, root CLI authority, planning checks, and closeout workflow.

invariants: Classification is descriptive, not permission to mutate.
Review-required and refused states remain review-required.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make lifecycle management straightforward, predictable, and low-judgment.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Agents can inspect why lifecycle surfaces changed, were preserved, were reviewed, or were refused from one structured output.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Legacy shape removal/refusal policy belongs to #483.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Read-only lifecycle outputs should not carry large classification payloads; the final implementation keeps classifications on mutation commands.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Lifecycle mutation JSON classifies surfaces by reason class and upgrade text points to JSON for detail.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Reingest and repeat until out of issues.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Continue the lifecycle hardening lane with #482 after #481 closed.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add derived root lifecycle surface classifications and tests.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Verify classification is compact, descriptive, and symmetric across dry-run/apply.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Root CLI lifecycle output/tests plus planning execplan/state.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused pytest, full workspace CLI pytest, ruff, proof selector, root CLI authority, planning checks.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Stop before rewriting module installers or adding a new lifecycle command.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Branch switching, destructive lifecycle policy, or generated package work.

stop_conditions.stop when: Classification tests and closeout proof pass.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The slice requires changing module ownership semantics.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The work becomes #483 legacy removal/refusal policy.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Dry-run/apply symmetry cannot be shown without broader behavior changes.

context_budget.live working set: #482, root lifecycle output code, lifecycle tests, active execplan.
context_budget.recoverable later: Historical reviews and unrelated derived follow-up backlog.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update this execplan with proof and closeout.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before commit or if classification scope expands into module installer policy.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Implement #482 end-to-end with root lifecycle surface classifications.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Stay on current branch, commit after proof, keep root lifecycle output compact.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Classification field names, reason taxonomy details, and test fixture arrangement.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A classification needs destructive automation or broad package-local rewrites.

active_milestone.scope: Root lifecycle output classification and tests.
active_milestone.ready: Issue #482 is open and #481 is closed.

immediate_next_action: Archive this plan, commit the milestone, close #482, refresh external evidence, then promote #483.
invariants: Text output remains compact and points to JSON for detail.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "surface_classifications or upgrade_text_output"
uv run ruff check src tests

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed src/agentic_workspace/cli.py src/agentic_workspace/workspace_output.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/lifecycle-output-surface-classification.plan.json --format json
uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section closeout_trust --format json
uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --task "dogfooding closeout review" --format json
completion_criteria: upgrade --dry-run --format json lists planned/preserved/refused/manual-review surfaces with reason classes.
Apply output uses the same classes as dry-run.
Tests cover core refresh, repo-owned preservation, optional legacy surface, local-only preservation, and ambiguous/refused surface.
Text output remains compact and points to JSON for detail.

execution_run.what happened: Added lifecycle_plan.surface_classifications for mutation commands, reason-class mapping over existing module/root report actions, local-only and optional-legacy scans, refused destructive classifications, and compact upgrade text routing to JSON detail.
execution_run.scope touched: Root lifecycle CLI output, text renderer, workspace CLI tests, and checked-in planning surfaces.
execution_run.changed surfaces: preflight, report, proof, reconcile, doctor, skills, root_cli_authority defaults, maintainer-surfaces.
execution_run.validations run: focused surface-classification pytest; full tests/test_workspace_cli.py; ruff; proof selector; root CLI authority; planning doctor; planning surface check; maintainer-surfaces; preflight; closeout_trust report; reconcile; skills dogfooding query.
execution_run.result for continuation: #482 is ready to close; lifecycle-management-hardening continues with #483 and #484.
execution_run.next step: Archive plan, commit, push, close #482, refresh external evidence, then continue #483.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; changes stayed in derived lifecycle output, text rendering, tests, and planning state.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; dry-run/apply classifications cover core refresh, repo-owned preservation, optional legacy, local-only, manual review, and refused destructive action.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used package workflow surfaces and stayed on the current branch.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; classifications are descriptive and do not authorize mutation.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: close #482 and continue lifecycle hardening with #483.

proof_report.validation proof: 201 workspace CLI tests passed; ruff passed; proof selector, root CLI authority, planning doctor, planning surface check, maintainer-surfaces, preflight, closeout_trust, reconcile, and skills query completed successfully.
proof_report.proof achieved now: Lifecycle mutation output classification works and upgrade text stays compact.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: #482 Classify lifecycle plan output by ownership, action, and reason.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: upgrade dry-run/apply JSON now includes lifecycle_plan.surface_classifications.entries with reason_class values; tests cover core refreshed, repo-owned preserved, optional legacy retained/removable, local-only preserved, ambiguous manual review, and refused destructive action; upgrade text points to JSON detail.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Add a compact derived field instead of new surfaces or prose.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does lifecycle output reduce judgment without increasing first-contact burden?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded slice is complete and remaining intent is routed to a checked-in continuation owner.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Prepared closeout records intent satisfaction, closure decision, proof evidence, and distillation buckets.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen when .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane lifecycle-management-hardening activates a fresh bounded slice.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Lifecycle mutation output now classifies relevant surfaces by ownership/action/reason.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused and full workspace CLI tests, ruff, proof selector, root CLI authority, planning doctor, planning surface check, maintainer-surfaces, preflight, closeout_trust, reconcile, and skills dogfooding query passed.

signals found: Read-only doctor/status output became too large when classifications were initially applied to every lifecycle command.
signals dismissed: Fixed in-slice by limiting surface classifications to mutation commands.
